tEn: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
tZh: Voyager：基于大型语言模型的开放式具身智能体
sEn: Voyager is the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that continuously explores, acquires skills, and makes discoveries without human intervention.
sZh: Voyager是首个由大型语言模型驱动的Minecraft具身终身学习智能体，无需人工干预即可持续探索、获取技能并做出新发现。
absEn: We introduce Voyager, the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that continuously explores the world, acquires diverse skills, and makes novel discoveries without human intervention. Voyager consists of three key components: 1) an automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration, 2) an ever-growing skill library of executable code for storing and retrieving complex behaviors, and 3) a new iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for program improvement. Voyager interacts with GPT-4 via blackbox queries, which bypasses the need for model parameter fine-tuning. The skills developed by Voyager are temporally extended, interpretable, and compositional, which compounds the agent's abilities rapidly and alleviates catastrophic forgetting. Empirically, Voyager shows strong in-context lifelong learning capability and exhibits exceptional proficiency in playing Minecraft. It obtains 3.3x more unique items, travels 2.3x longer distances, and unlocks key tech tree milestones up to 15.3x faster than prior SOTA. Voyager is able to utilize the learned skill library in a new Minecraft world to solve novel tasks from scratch, while other techniques struggle to generalize. We open-source our full codebase and prompts at https://voyager.minedojo.org/.
absZh: 我们介绍了Voyager，这是首个在Minecraft中由大型语言模型驱动的具身终身学习智能体，它能够持续探索世界、获取多样技能，并在无需人工干预的情况下做出新颖发现。Voyager由三个关键组件构成：1）最大化探索的自动课程；2）不断增长的技能库，用于存储和检索可执行代码的复杂行为；3）一种新的迭代提示机制，结合环境反馈、执行错误和自验证来改进程序。Voyager通过黑盒查询与GPT-4交互，无需微调模型参数。Voyager开发的技能具有时间延展性、可解释性和组合性，这迅速增强了智能体的能力并减轻了灾难性遗忘。实验表明，Voyager展现出强大的上下文终身学习能力，并在玩Minecraft方面表现出色。它获得的独特物品数量是先前最先进方法的3.3倍，旅行距离是其2.3倍，解锁关键技术树里程碑的速度快达15.3倍。Voyager能够利用学到的技能库在新的Minecraft世界中从头解决新任务，而其他技术难以泛化。我们在https://voyager.minedojo.org/开源了完整的代码库和提示。

contrib.en: First LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft, exploring and acquiring skills without human intervention.
contrib.zh: 首个由大语言模型驱动的具身终身学习智能体，在Minecraft中无需人工干预即可探索世界并获取技能。

contrib.en: Automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration by proposing tasks based on agent state and progress.
contrib.zh: 自动课程最大化探索，根据智能体状态和进展提出任务。

contrib.en: Growing skill library of executable code for storing and retrieving complex, compositional behaviors.
contrib.zh: 不断增长的技能库，存储和检索可执行代码的复杂组合行为。

contrib.en: Iterative prompting mechanism using environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for code improvement.
contrib.zh: 迭代提示机制，利用环境反馈、执行错误和自我验证改进代码。

contrib.en: Outperforms prior SOTA: 3.3x more unique items, 2.3x longer distances, 15.3x faster tech tree milestones.
contrib.zh: 超越先前最优：3.3倍独特物品、2.3倍移动距离、15.3倍更快解锁科技树里程碑。

contrib.en: Zero-shot generalization to novel tasks in new worlds using learned skill library.
contrib.zh: 零样本泛化到新世界中的新任务，利用已学技能库。

limits.en: No visual perception; relies on text-only GPT-4 API, limiting spatial tasks.
limits.zh: 缺乏视觉感知，依赖纯文本GPT-4 API，限制空间任务。

limits.en: High cost of GPT-4 API; 15x more expensive than GPT-3.5.
Occasional failures in code generation and self-verification, e.g., hallucinating non-existent items.
limits.zh: 代码生成和自我验证偶尔失败，例如幻觉出不存在的物品。

limits.en: Automatic curriculum may propose unachievable tasks due to hallucinations.
limits.zh: 自动课程可能因幻觉提出无法完成的任务。

limits.en: Requires high-level Mineflayer API; not directly comparable to low-level pixel-based methods.
limits.zh: 需要高级Mineflayer API，无法直接与基于像素的低级方法比较。

paras.en: We introduce Voyager, the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that continuously explores the world, acquires diverse skills, and makes novel discoveries without human intervention. Voyager consists of three key components: 1) an automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration, 2) an ever-growing skill library of executable code for storing and retrieving complex behaviors, and 3) a new iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for program improvement. Voyager interacts with GPT-4 via blackbox queries, which bypasses the need for model parameter fine-tuning. The skills developed by Voyager are temporally extended, interpretable, and compositional, which compounds the agent’s abilities rapidly and alleviates catastrophic forgetting. Empirically, Voyager shows strong in-context lifelong learning capability and exhibits exceptional proficiency in playing Minecraft. It obtains 3.3×3.3\times more unique items, travels 2.3×2.3\times longer distances, and unlocks key tech tree milestones up to 15.3×15.3\times faster than prior SOTA. Voyager is able to utilize the learned skill library in a new Minecraft world to solve novel tasks from scratch, while other techniques struggle to generalize.
paras.zh: 我们提出了Voyager，这是第一个在Minecraft中由LLM驱动的具身终身学习智能体，它能够持续探索世界、获取多样技能并在无需人工干预的情况下做出新发现。Voyager由三个关键组件构成：1）最大化探索的自动课程；2）一个不断增长的、用于存储和检索复杂行为的可执行代码技能库；3）一种新的迭代提示机制，该机制结合环境反馈、执行错误和自我验证来改进程序。Voyager通过黑盒查询与GPT-4交互，无需微调模型参数。Voyager开发的技能具有时间延展性、可解释性和组合性，这迅速增强了智能体的能力并缓解了灾难性遗忘。实验表明，Voyager展现出强大的上下文终身学习能力，并在玩Minecraft方面表现出色。它获得的独特物品数量是先前最先进方法的3.3倍，旅行距离是其2.3倍，解锁关键技术树里程碑的速度最高提升15.3倍。Voyager能够在一个新的Minecraft世界中利用已学习的技能库从头开始解决新任务，而其他技术难以泛化。

paras.en: Building generally capable embodied agents that continuously explore, plan, and develop new skills in open-ended worlds is a grand challenge for the AI community [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]. Classical approaches employ reinforcement learning (RL) [6, 7] and imitation learning [8, 9, 10] that operate on primitive actions, which could be challenging for systematic exploration [11, 12, 13, 14, 15], interpretability [16, 17, 18], and generalization [19, 20, 21]. Recent advances in large language model (LLM) based agents harness the world knowledge encapsulated in pre-trained LLMs to generate consistent action plans or executable policies [16, 22, 19]. They are applied to embodied tasks like games and robotics [23, 24, 25, 26, 27], as well as NLP tasks without embodiment [28, 29, 30]. However, these agents are not lifelong learners that can progressively acquire, update, accumulate, and transfer knowledge over extended time spans [31, 32].
paras.zh: 构建能够在开放世界中持续探索、规划并发展新技能的通用具身智能体，是人工智能领域的一项重大挑战[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]。传统方法采用基于原始动作的强化学习[6, 7]和模仿学习[8, 9, 10]，这在系统性探索[11, 12, 13, 14, 15]、可解释性[16, 17, 18]和泛化[19, 20, 21]方面可能面临挑战。近期基于大语言模型的智能体利用预训练LLM中蕴含的世界知识来生成一致的动作计划或可执行策略[16, 22, 19]，并应用于游戏和机器人等具身任务[23, 24, 25, 26, 27]，以及非具身的NLP任务[28, 29, 30]。然而，这些智能体并非终身学习者，无法在长时间跨度内逐步获取、更新、积累和迁移知识[31, 32]。

paras.en: Let us consider Minecraft as an example. Unlike most other games studied in AI [33, 34, 10], Minecraft does not impose a predefined end goal or a fixed storyline but rather provides a unique playground with endless possibilities [23]. Minecraft requires players to explore vast, procedurally generated 3D terrains and unlock a tech tree using gathered resources. Human players typically start by learning the basics, such as mining wood and cooking food, before advancing to more complex tasks like combating monsters and crafting diamond tools. We argue that an effective lifelong learning agent should have similar capabilities as human players: (1) propose suitable tasks based on its current skill level and world state, e.g., learn to harvest sand and cactus before iron if it finds itself in a desert rather than a forest; (2) refine skills based on environmental feedback and commit mastered skills to memory for future reuse in similar situations (e.g. fighting zombies is similar to fighting spiders); (3) continually explore the world and seek out new tasks in a self-driven manner.
paras.zh: 以《我的世界》为例。与AI研究中大多数其他游戏不同[33, 34, 10]，《我的世界》没有预设的最终目标或固定剧情，而是提供了一个充满无限可能的独特游乐场[23]。《我的世界》要求玩家探索广阔的程序生成3D地形，并利用收集的资源解锁科技树。人类玩家通常从学习基础知识开始，例如挖木和烹饪食物，然后进阶到更复杂的任务，如对抗怪物和制作钻石工具。我们认为，一个有效的终身学习智能体应具备与人类玩家相似的能力：(1) 根据当前技能水平和世界状态提出合适的任务，例如，如果发现自己身处沙漠而非森林，则先学习收获沙子和仙人掌，再学习铁器；(2) 基于环境反馈完善技能，并将已掌握的技能存入记忆，以便将来在类似情境中复用（例如，与僵尸战斗类似于与蜘蛛战斗）；(3) 持续探索世界，并以自我驱动的方式寻找新任务。

paras.en: Towards these goals, we introduce Voyager, the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent to drive exploration, master a wide range of skills, and make new discoveries continually without human intervention in Minecraft. Voyager is made possible through three key modules (Fig. 2): 1) an automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration; 2) a skill library for storing and retrieving complex behaviors; and 3) a new iterative prompting mechanism that generates executable code for embodied control. We opt to use code as the action space instead of low-level motor commands because programs can naturally represent temporally extended and compositional actions [16, 22], which are essential for many long-horizon tasks in Minecraft. Voyager interacts with a blackbox LLM (GPT-4 [35]) through prompting and in-context learning [36, 37, 38]. Our approach bypasses the need for model parameter access and explicit gradient-based training or finetuning.
paras.zh: 为实现这些目标，我们引入了Voyager——首个由LLM驱动的具身终身学习智能体，能够在《我的世界》中无需人工干预地推动探索、掌握广泛技能并持续做出新发现。Voyager通过三个关键模块实现（图2）：1) 最大化探索的自动课程；2) 用于存储和检索复杂行为的技能库；3) 一种新的迭代提示机制，用于生成具身控制的可执行代码。我们选择使用代码作为动作空间，而非低级运动指令，因为程序能够自然地表示时间延展和组合动作[16, 22]，这对于《我的世界》中的许多长时域任务至关重要。Voyager通过提示和上下文学习[36, 37, 38]与黑盒LLM（GPT-4 [35]）交互。我们的方法无需访问模型参数，也无需显式的基于梯度的训练或微调。

paras.en: More specifically, Voyager attempts to solve progressively harder tasks proposed by the automatic curriculum, which takes into account the exploration progress and the agent’s state. The curriculum is generated by GPT-4 based on the overarching goal of “discovering as many diverse things as possible”. This approach can be perceived as an in-context form of novelty search [39, 40]. Voyager incrementally builds a skill library by storing the action programs that help solve a task successfully. Each program is indexed by the embedding of its description, which can be retrieved in similar situations in the future. Complex skills can be synthesized by composing simpler programs, which compounds Voyager’s capabilities rapidly over time and alleviates catastrophic forgetting in other continual learning methods [31, 32].
paras.zh: 更具体地说，Voyager尝试解决由自动课程提出的逐步困难的任务，该课程考虑了探索进度和智能体的状态。课程由GPT-4基于“发现尽可能多不同事物”的总体目标生成。这种方法可被视为一种上下文形式的新颖性搜索[39, 40]。Voyager通过存储成功解决任务的行动程序，逐步构建技能库。每个程序由其描述的嵌入索引，以便在未来类似情境中检索。复杂技能可以通过组合简单程序来合成，这使Voyager的能力随时间快速累积，并缓解了其他持续学习方法中的灾难性遗忘[31, 32]。

paras.en: However, LLMs struggle to produce the correct action code consistently in one shot [41]. To address this challenge, we propose an iterative prompting mechanism that: (1) executes the generated program to obtain observations from the Minecraft simulation (such as inventory listing and nearby creatures) and error trace from the code interpreter (if any); (2) incorporates the feedback into GPT-4’s prompt for another round of code refinement; and (3) repeats the process until a self-verification module confirms the task completion, at which point we commit the program to the skill library (e.g., craftStoneShovel() and combatZombieWithSword()) and query the automatic curriculum for the next milestone (Fig. 2).
paras.zh: 然而，LLM难以一次性持续生成正确的动作代码[41]。为应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种迭代提示机制：(1) 执行生成的程序，从《我的世界》模拟中获取观察（如库存列表和附近生物）以及代码解释器的错误追踪（如有）；(2) 将反馈纳入GPT-4的提示，进行另一轮代码改进；(3) 重复该过程，直到自我验证模块确认任务完成，此时我们将程序提交到技能库（例如，craftStoneShovel()和combatZombieWithSword()），并查询自动课程以获取下一个里程碑（图2）。

paras.en: Empirically, Voyager demonstrates strong in-context lifelong learning capabilities. It can construct an ever-growing skill library of action programs that are reusable, interpretable, and generalizable to novel tasks. We evaluate Voyager systematically against other LLM-based agent techniques (e.g., ReAct [29], Reflexion [30], AutoGPT [28]) in MineDojo [23], an open-source Minecraft AI framework. Voyager outperforms prior SOTA by obtaining 3.3×3.3\times more unique items, unlocking key tech tree milestones up to 15.3×15.3\times faster, and traversing 2.3×2.3\times longer distances. We further demonstrate that Voyager is able to utilize the learned skill library in a new Minecraft world to solve novel tasks from scratch, while other methods struggle to generalize.
paras.zh: 实验表明，Voyager展现出强大的上下文终身学习能力。它能够构建一个不断增长的动作程序技能库，这些程序可复用、可解释，并能泛化到新任务。我们在MineDojo[23]（一个开源的《我的世界》AI框架）中，将Voyager与其他基于LLM的智能体技术（如ReAct[29]、Reflexion[30]、AutoGPT[28]）进行了系统比较。Voyager在获取独特物品数量上超越先前最先进方法3.3倍，解锁关键科技树里程碑的速度提升高达15.3倍，探索距离增加2.3倍。我们进一步证明，Voyager能够利用已学习的技能库在新的《我的世界》世界中从头解决新任务，而其他方法难以泛化。

paras.en: Voyager consists of three novel components: (1) an automatic curriculum (Sec. 2.1) that suggests objectives for open-ended exploration, (2) a skill library (Sec. 2.2) for developing increasingly complex behaviors, and (3) an iterative prompting mechanism (Sec. 2.3) that generates executable code for embodied control. Full prompts are presented in Appendix, Sec. A.
paras.zh: Voyager 包含三个新颖的组件：(1) 自动课程（第 2.1 节），为开放式探索建议目标；(2) 技能库（第 2.2 节），用于发展日益复杂的行为；(3) 迭代提示机制（第 2.3 节），生成用于具身控制的可执行代码。完整提示见附录第 A 节。

paras.en: Embodied agents encounter a variety of objectives with different complexity levels in open-ended environments. An automatic curriculum offers numerous benefits for open-ended exploration, ensuring a challenging but manageable learning process, fostering curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation for agents to learn and explore, and encouraging the development of general and flexible problem-solving strategies [42, 43, 44]. Our automatic curriculum capitalizes on the internet-scale knowledge contained within GPT-4 by prompting it to provide a steady stream of new tasks or challenges. The curriculum unfolds in a bottom-up fashion, allowing for considerable adaptability and responsiveness to the exploration progress and the agent’s current state (Fig. 3). As Voyager progresses to harder self-driven goals, it naturally learns a variety of skills, such as “mining a diamond”.
paras.zh: 具身智能体在开放式环境中会遇到各种不同复杂程度的目标。自动课程为开放式探索提供了诸多好处，确保学习过程具有挑战性但可控，激发智能体基于好奇心的内在动机去学习和探索，并鼓励发展通用且灵活的问题解决策略[42, 43, 44]。我们的自动课程利用GPT-4中包含的互联网规模知识，通过提示它提供源源不断的新任务或挑战。课程以自底向上的方式展开，允许对探索进度和智能体当前状态具有相当大的适应性和响应性（图3）。随着Voyager向更难的自我驱动目标前进，它自然地学习各种技能，例如“挖掘钻石”。

paras.en: The input prompt to GPT-4 consists of several components:
paras.zh: 输入给GPT-4的提示包含几个组成部分：

paras.en: Directives encouraging diverse behaviors and imposing constraints, such as “My ultimate goal is to discover as many diverse things as possible ... The next task should not be too hard since I may not have the necessary resources or have learned enough skills to complete it yet.”;
paras.zh: 鼓励多样化行为并施加约束的指令，例如“我的最终目标是发现尽可能多不同的事物……下一个任务不应太难，因为我可能还没有必要的资源或学到足够的技能来完成它。”；

paras.en: The agent’s current state, including inventory, equipment, nearby blocks and entities, biome, time, health and hunger bars, and position;
paras.zh: 智能体的当前状态，包括库存、装备、附近的方块和实体、生物群系、时间、生命值和饥饿值条以及位置；

paras.en: Previously completed and failed tasks, reflecting the agent’s current exploration progress and capabilities frontier;
paras.zh: 先前完成和失败的任务，反映智能体当前的探索进度和能力边界；

paras.en: Additional context: We also leverage GPT-3.5 to self-ask questions based on the agent’s current state and exploration progress and self-answer questions. We opt to use GPT-3.5 instead of GPT-4 for standard NLP tasks due to budgetary considerations.
paras.zh: 额外上下文：我们还利用GPT-3.5基于智能体的当前状态和探索进度自我提问并自我回答问题。出于预算考虑，我们选择使用GPT-3.5而不是GPT-4来处理标准NLP任务。

paras.en: With the automatic curriculum consistently proposing increasingly complex tasks, it is essential to have a skill library that serves as a basis for learning and evolution. Inspired by the generality, interpretability, and universality of programs [45], we represent each skill with executable code that scaffolds temporally extended actions for completing a specific task proposed by the automatic curriculum.
paras.zh: 随着自动课程持续提出日益复杂的任务，拥有一个作为学习和进化基础的技能库至关重要。受程序的一般性、可解释性和通用性启发[45]，我们将每个技能表示为可执行代码，这些代码支撑了完成自动课程提出的特定任务的时域扩展动作。

paras.en: The input prompt to GPT-4 consists of the following components:
paras.zh: 输入给GPT-4的提示包含以下组成部分：

paras.en: Guidelines for code generation, such as “Your function will be reused for building more complex functions. Therefore, you should make it generic and reusable.”;
paras.zh: 代码生成指南，例如“你的函数将被重用于构建更复杂的函数。因此，你应该使其通用且可重用。”；

paras.en: Control primitive APIs, and relevant skills retrieved from the skill library, which are crucial for in-context learning [36, 37, 38] to work well;
paras.zh: 控制原始API以及从技能库中检索的相关技能，这些对于上下文学习[36, 37, 38]的有效运作至关重要；

paras.en: The generated code from the last round, environment feedback, execution errors, and critique, based on which GPT-4 can self-improve (Sec. 2.3);
paras.zh: 上一轮生成的代码、环境反馈、执行错误和批评，基于这些GPT-4可以进行自我改进（第2.3节）；

paras.en: The agent’s current state, including inventory, equipment, nearby blocks and entities, biome, time, health and hunger bars, and position;
paras.zh: 智能体的当前状态，包括库存、装备、附近的方块和实体、生物群系、时间、生命值和饥饿值条以及位置；

paras.en: Chain-of-thought prompting [46] to do reasoning before code generation.
paras.zh: 思维链提示[46]在代码生成前进行推理。

paras.en: We iteratively refine the program through a novel iterative prompting mechanism (Sec. 2.3), incorporate it into the skill library as a new skill, and index it by the embedding of its description (Fig. 4, top). For skill retrieval, we query the skill library with the embedding of self-generated task plans and environment feedback (Fig. 4, bottom). By continuously expanding and refining the skill library, Voyager can learn, adapt, and excel in a wide spectrum of tasks, consistently pushing the boundaries of its capabilities in the open world.
paras.zh: 我们通过一种新颖的迭代提示机制（第2.3节）迭代地完善程序，将其作为新技能纳入技能库，并通过其描述的嵌入进行索引（图4，顶部）。对于技能检索，我们使用自生成任务计划和环境反馈的嵌入来查询技能库（图4，底部）。通过不断扩展和完善技能库，Voyager可以在广泛的任务中学习、适应并表现出色，持续推动其在开放世界中能力的边界。

paras.en: We introduce an iterative prompting mechanism for self-improvement through three types of feedback:
paras.zh: 我们引入了一种通过三种反馈进行自我改进的迭代提示机制：

paras.en: Environment feedback, which illustrates the intermediate progress of program execution (Fig. 5, left). For example, “I cannot make an iron chestplate because I need: 7 more iron ingots” highlights the cause of failure in crafting an iron chestplate. We use bot.chat() inside control primitive APIs to generate environment feedback and prompt GPT-4 to use this function as well during code generation;
paras.zh: 环境反馈，它说明了程序执行的中间进展（图5左）。例如，“我无法制作铁胸甲，因为我需要：7个更多铁锭”突出了制作铁胸甲失败的原因。我们在控制原语API中使用bot.chat()来生成环境反馈，并提示GPT-4在代码生成过程中也使用此函数；

paras.en: Execution errors from the program interpreter that reveal any invalid operations or syntax errors in programs, which are valuable for bug fixing (Fig. 5, right);
paras.zh: 程序解释器产生的执行错误，揭示了程序中任何无效操作或语法错误，这些对修复错误很有价值（图5右）；

paras.en: Self-verification for checking task success. Instead of manually coding success checkers for each new task proposed by the automatic curriculum, we instantiate another GPT-4 agent for self-verification. By providing Voyager’s current state and the task to GPT-4, we ask it to act as a critic [47, 48, 49] and inform us whether the program achieves the task. In addition, if the task fails, it provides a critique by suggesting how to complete the task (Fig. 6). Hence, our self-verification is more comprehensive than self-reflection [30] by both checking success and reflecting on mistakes.
paras.zh: 用于检查任务成功的自我验证。我们不是为自动课程提出的每个新任务手动编写成功检查器，而是实例化另一个GPT-4智能体进行自我验证。通过向GPT-4提供Voyager的当前状态和任务，我们要求它充当评论者[47, 48, 49]，并告知我们程序是否完成了任务。此外，如果任务失败，它会通过建议如何完成任务来提供批评（图6）。因此，我们的自我验证比自我反思[30]更全面，既检查成功又反思错误。

paras.en: During each round of code generation, we execute the generated program to obtain environment feedback and execution errors from the code interpreter, which are incorporated into GPT-4’s prompt for the next round of code refinement. This iterative process repeats until self-verification validates the task’s completion, at which point we add this new skill to the skill library and ask the automatic curriculum for a new objective (Fig. 2). If the agent gets stuck after 4 rounds of code generation, then we query the curriculum for another task. This iterative prompting approach significantly improves program synthesis for embodied control, enabling Voyager to continuously acquire diverse skills without human intervention.
paras.zh: 在每轮代码生成中，我们执行生成的程序以从代码解释器获取环境反馈和执行错误，这些被纳入GPT-4的提示中，用于下一轮代码改进。这个迭代过程重复进行，直到自我验证确认任务完成，此时我们将这个新技能添加到技能库中，并请求自动课程提供新目标（图2）。如果智能体在4轮代码生成后陷入困境，则我们向课程查询另一个任务。这种迭代提示方法显著改进了用于具身控制的程序合成，使Voyager能够在无需人工干预的情况下持续获取多样技能。

paras.en: We leverage OpenAI’s gpt-4-0314 [35] and gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 [50] APIs for text completion, along with text-embedding-ada-002 [51] API for text embedding. We set all temperatures to 0 except for the automatic curriculum, which uses temperature == 0.1 to encourage task diversity. Our simulation environment is built on top of MineDojo [23] and leverages Mineflayer [52] JavaScript APIs for motor controls. See Appendix, Sec. B.1 for more details.
paras.zh: 我们利用 OpenAI 的 gpt-4-0314 [35] 和 gpt-3.5-turbo-0301 [50] API 进行文本补全，以及 text-embedding-ada-002 [51] API 进行文本嵌入。除自动课程外，所有温度参数均设为 0，自动课程使用 temperature == 0.1 以鼓励任务多样性。我们的模拟环境基于 MineDojo [23] 构建，并利用 Mineflayer [52] JavaScript API 进行运动控制。详见附录 B.1 节。

paras.en: Because there is no LLM-based agents that work out of the box for Minecraft, we make our best effort to select a number of representative algorithms as baselines. These methods are originally designed only for NLP tasks without embodiment, therefore we have to re-interpret them to be executable in MineDojo and compatible with our experimental setting:
paras.zh: 由于目前没有现成的基于LLM的智能体可用于Minecraft，我们尽力选择了一些代表性算法作为基线。这些方法最初仅针对NLP任务设计，不具备具身性，因此我们必须重新解释它们，使其能够在MineDojo中执行并兼容我们的实验设置：

paras.en: ReAct [29] uses chain-of-thought prompting [46] by generating both reasoning traces and action plans with LLMs. We provide it with our environment feedback and the agent states as observations.
paras.zh: ReAct [29] 使用思维链提示 [46]，通过LLM生成推理轨迹和行动计划。我们为其提供环境反馈和智能体状态作为观察。

paras.en: Reflexion [30] is built on top of ReAct [29] with self-reflection to infer more intuitive future actions. We provide it with execution errors and our self-verification module.
paras.zh: Reflexion [30] 基于ReAct [29]构建，通过自我反思推断更直观的未来行动。我们为其提供执行错误和我们的自我验证模块。

paras.en: AutoGPT [28] is a popular software tool that automates NLP tasks by decomposing a high-level goal into multiple subgoals and executing them in a ReAct-style loop. We re-implement AutoGPT by using GPT-4 to do task decomposition and provide it with the agent states, environment feedback, and execution errors as observations for subgoal execution. Compared with Voyager, AutoGPT lacks the skill library for accumulating knowledge, self-verification for assessing task success, and automatic curriculum for open-ended exploration.
paras.zh: AutoGPT [28] 是一种流行的软件工具，通过将高级目标分解为多个子目标并以ReAct风格的循环执行来自动化NLP任务。我们重新实现了AutoGPT，使用GPT-4进行任务分解，并为其提供智能体状态、环境反馈和执行错误作为子目标执行的观察。与Voyager相比，AutoGPT缺乏用于积累知识的技能库、用于评估任务成功的自我验证以及用于开放式探索的自动课程。

paras.en: Note that we do not directly compare with prior methods that take Minecraft screen pixels as input and output low-level controls [53, 54, 55]. It would not be an apple-to-apple comparison, because we rely on the high-level Mineflayer [52] API to control the agent. Our work’s focus is on pushing the limits of GPT-4 for lifelong embodied agent learning, rather than solving the 3D perception or sensorimotor control problems. Voyager is orthogonal and can be combined with gradient-based approaches like VPT [8] as long as the controller provides a code API. We make a system-level comparison between Voyager and prior Minecraft agents in Table. A.2.
paras.zh: 请注意，我们不直接与先前以Minecraft屏幕像素为输入并输出低级控制的方法 [53, 54, 55] 进行比较。这将不是公平的比较，因为我们依赖高级Mineflayer [52] API来控制智能体。我们的工作重点是推动GPT-4在终身具身智能体学习中的极限，而不是解决3D感知或感觉运动控制问题。Voyager是正交的，只要控制器提供代码API，就可以与基于梯度的方法（如VPT [8]）结合。我们在表A.2中对Voyager与先前的Minecraft智能体进行了系统级比较。

paras.en: We systematically evaluate Voyager and baselines on their exploration performance, tech tree mastery, map coverage, and zero-shot generalization capability to novel tasks in a new world.
paras.zh: 我们系统地评估了Voyager和基线方法在探索性能、科技树掌握、地图覆盖以及在新世界中零样本泛化到新任务的能力。

paras.en: Significantly better exploration. Results of exploration performance are shown in Fig. 1. Voyager’s superiority is evident in its ability to consistently make new strides, discovering 63 unique items within 160 prompting iterations, 3.3×3.3\times many novel items compared to its counterparts. On the other hand, AutoGPT lags considerably in discovering new items, while ReAct and Reflexion struggle to make significant progress, given the abstract nature of the open-ended exploration goal that is challenging to execute without an appropriate curriculum.
paras.zh: 显著更好的探索。探索性能的结果如图1所示。Voyager的优势体现在其能够持续取得新进展，在160次提示迭代内发现了63个独特物品，是同类方法的3.3倍。相比之下，AutoGPT在发现新物品方面明显滞后，而ReAct和Reflexion由于开放探索目标的抽象性，在没有适当课程的情况下难以执行，因此进展甚微。

paras.en: Consistent tech tree mastery. The Minecraft tech tree tests the agent’s ability to craft and use a hierarchy of tools. Progressing through this tree (wooden tool →→\rightarrow stone tool →→\rightarrow iron tool →→\rightarrow diamond tool) requires the agent to master systematic and compositional skills. Compared with baselines, Voyager unlocks the wooden level 15.3×15.3\times faster (in terms of the prompting iterations), the stone level 8.5×8.5\times faster, the iron level 6.4×6.4\times faster, and Voyager is the only one to unlock the diamond level of the tech tree (Fig. 2 and Table. 1). This underscores the effectiveness of the automatic curriculum, which consistently presents challenges of suitable complexity to facilitate the agent’s progress.
paras.zh: 一致的科技树掌握。Minecraft科技树测试智能体制作和使用工具层次结构的能力。沿着该树前进（木制工具→石制工具→铁制工具→钻石工具）需要智能体掌握系统性和组合性技能。与基线方法相比，Voyager解锁木制等级的速度快15.3倍（以提示迭代次数计），石制等级快8.5倍，铁制等级快6.4倍，并且Voyager是唯一解锁科技树钻石等级的智能体（图2和表1）。这凸显了自动课程的有效性，它持续提供适当复杂度的挑战，以促进智能体的进步。

paras.en: Extensive map traversal. Voyager is able to navigate distances 2.3×2.3\times longer compared to baselines by traversing a variety of terrains, while the baseline agents often find themselves confined to local areas, which significantly hampers their capacity to discover new knowledge (Fig. 7).
paras.zh: 广泛的地图遍历。Voyager能够穿越各种地形，导航距离比基线方法长2.3倍，而基线智能体往往局限于局部区域，这严重阻碍了它们发现新知识的能力（图7）。

paras.en: Efficient zero-shot generalization to unseen tasks. To evaluate zero-shot generalization, we clear the agent’s inventory, reset it to a newly instantiated world, and test it with unseen tasks. For both Voyager and AutoGPT, we utilize GPT-4 to break down the task into a series of subgoals. Table. 2 and Fig. 8 show Voyager can consistently solve all the tasks, while baselines cannot solve any task within 50 prompting iterations. What’s interesting to note is that our skill library constructed from lifelong learning not only enhances Voyager’s performance but also gives a boost to AutoGPT. This demonstrates that the skill library serves as a versatile tool that can be readily employed by other methods, effectively acting as a plug-and-play asset to enhance performance.
paras.zh: 对未见任务的高效零样本泛化。为了评估零样本泛化，我们清空智能体的库存，将其重置到新实例化的世界，并用未见任务进行测试。对于Voyager和AutoGPT，我们都使用GPT-4将任务分解为一系列子目标。表2和图8显示，Voyager能够一致地解决所有任务，而基线方法在50次提示迭代内无法解决任何任务。有趣的是，我们从终身学习中构建的技能库不仅提升了Voyager的性能，也增强了AutoGPT的表现。这表明技能库作为一种多功能工具，可以被其他方法轻松采用，有效地作为即插即用的资产来提升性能。

paras.en: We ablate 6 design choices (automatic curriculum, skill library, environment feedback, execution errors, self-verification, and GPT-4 for code generation) in Voyager and study their impact on exploration performance (see Appendix, Sec. B.3 for details of each ablated variant). Results are shown in Fig. 9. We highlight the key findings below:
paras.zh: 我们对 Voyager 中的 6 个设计选择（自动课程、技能库、环境反馈、执行错误、自我验证和 GPT-4 代码生成）进行了消融实验，并研究了它们对探索性能的影响（每种消融变体的详细信息见附录 B.3 节）。结果如图 9 所示。以下重点介绍主要发现：

paras.en: Automatic curriculum is crucial for the agent’s consistent progress. The discovered item count drops by 93%percent9393\% if the curriculum is replaced with a random one, because certain tasks may be too challenging if attempted out of order. On the other hand, a manually designed curriculum requires significant Minecraft-specific expertise, and does not take into account the agent’s live situation. It falls short in the experimental results compared to our automatic curriculum.
paras.zh: 自动课程对于智能体的持续进步至关重要。如果将课程替换为随机课程，发现物品的数量会下降 93%，因为某些任务如果顺序不当可能过于困难。另一方面，手动设计的课程需要大量的 Minecraft 专业知识，并且不考虑智能体的实时情况。在实验结果中，它不如我们的自动课程。

paras.en: Voyager w/o skill library exhibits a tendency to plateau in the later stages. This underscores the pivotal role that the skill library plays in Voyager. It helps create more complex actions and steadily pushes the agent’s boundaries by encouraging new skills to be built upon older ones.
paras.zh: 没有技能库的 Voyager 在后期阶段表现出趋于平稳的趋势。这凸显了技能库在 Voyager 中的关键作用。它有助于创建更复杂的动作，并通过鼓励在旧技能基础上构建新技能来稳步推动智能体的边界。

paras.en: Self-verification is the most important among all the feedback types. Removing the module leads to a significant drop (−73%percent73-73\%) in the discovered item count. Self-verification serves as a critical mechanism to decide when to move on to a new task or reattempt a previously unsuccessful task.
paras.zh: 自我验证是所有反馈类型中最重要的。移除该模块会导致发现物品数量显著下降（-73%）。自我验证是一个关键机制，用于决定何时开始新任务或重试先前未成功的任务。

paras.en: GPT-4 significantly outperforms GPT-3.5 in code generation and obtains 5.7×5.7\times more unique items, as GPT-4 exhibits a quantum leap in coding abilities. This finding corroborates recent studies in the literature [56, 57].
paras.zh: GPT-4 在代码生成方面显著优于 GPT-3.5，获得的独特物品数量是后者的 5.7 倍，因为 GPT-4 在编码能力上表现出量子跃迁。这一发现与文献 [56, 57] 中的近期研究一致。

full.secZh: 3.5 来自人类的多模态反馈

paras.en: Voyager does not currently support visual perception, because the available version of GPT-4 API is text-only at the time of this writing. However, Voyager has the potential to be augmented by multimodal perception models [58, 59] to achieve more impressive tasks. We demonstrate that given human feedback, Voyager is able to construct complex 3D structures in Minecraft, such as a Nether Portal and a house (Fig. 10). There are two ways to integrate human feedback:
paras.zh: Voyager 目前不支持视觉感知，因为本文撰写时可用的 GPT-4 API 仅支持文本。然而，Voyager 有潜力通过多模态感知模型 [58, 59] 增强，以完成更令人印象深刻的任务。我们证明，在给定人类反馈的情况下，Voyager 能够在 Minecraft 中构建复杂的 3D 结构，例如下界传送门和房屋（图 10）。集成人类反馈有两种方式：

paras.en: Human as a critic (equivalent to Voyager’s self-verification module): humans provide visual critique to Voyager, allowing it to modify the code from the previous round. This feedback is essential for correcting certain errors in the spatial details of a 3D structure that Voyager cannot perceive directly.
paras.zh: 人类作为批评者（相当于 Voyager 的自我验证模块）：人类向 Voyager 提供视觉批评，使其能够修改上一轮的代码。这种反馈对于纠正 Voyager 无法直接感知的 3D 结构空间细节中的某些错误至关重要。

paras.en: Human as a curriculum (equivalent to Voyager’s automatic curriculum module): humans break down a complex building task into smaller steps, guiding Voyager to complete them incrementally. This approach improves Voyager’s ability to handle more sophisticated 3D construction tasks.
paras.zh: 人类作为课程（相当于 Voyager 的自动课程模块）：人类将复杂的建筑任务分解为更小的步骤，引导 Voyager 逐步完成。这种方法提高了 Voyager 处理更复杂的 3D 建筑任务的能力。

paras.en: Cost. The GPT-4 API incurs significant costs. It is 15×15\times more expensive than GPT-3.5. Nevertheless, Voyager requires the quantum leap in code generation quality from GPT-4 (Fig. 9), which GPT-3.5 and open-source LLMs cannot provide [60].
paras.zh: 成本。GPT-4 API 成本高昂，比 GPT-3.5 贵 15×15\times 倍。然而，Voyager 需要 GPT-4 在代码生成质量上的巨大飞跃（图 9），这是 GPT-3.5 和开源大语言模型无法提供的 [60]。

paras.en: Inaccuracies. Despite the iterative prompting mechanism, there are still cases where the agent gets stuck and fails to generate the correct skill. The automatic curriculum has the flexibility to reattempt this task at a later time. Occasionally, self-verification module may also fail, such as not recognizing spider string as a success signal of beating a spider.
paras.zh: 不准确性。尽管采用了迭代提示机制，但仍有智能体陷入困境、无法生成正确技能的情况。自动课程具有灵活性，可以在稍后重试此任务。偶尔，自我验证模块也可能失败，例如未能将蜘蛛网识别为击败蜘蛛的成功信号。

paras.en: Hallucinations. The automatic curriculum occasionally proposes unachievable tasks. For example, it may ask the agent to craft a “copper sword" or “copper chestplate", which are items that do not exist within the game. Hallucinations also occur during the code generation process. For instance, GPT-4 tends to use cobblestone as a fuel input, despite being an invalid fuel source in the game. Additionally, it may call functions absent in the provided control primitive APIs, leading to code execution errors.
paras.zh: 幻觉。自动课程偶尔会提出无法实现的任务。例如，它可能要求智能体制作“铜剑”或“铜胸甲”，而这些物品在游戏中并不存在。代码生成过程中也会出现幻觉。例如，GPT-4 倾向于使用圆石作为燃料输入，尽管它在游戏中是无效的燃料来源。此外，它可能调用提供的控制原始 API 中不存在的函数，导致代码执行错误。

paras.en: We are confident that improvements in the GPT API models as well as novel techniques for finetuning open-source LLMs will overcome these limitations in the future.
paras.zh: 我们相信，GPT API 模型的改进以及微调开源大语言模型的新技术将在未来克服这些局限性。

paras.en: Minecraft is an open-ended 3D world with incredibly flexible game mechanics supporting a broad spectrum of activities. Built upon notable Minecraft benchmarks [23, 61, 62, 63, 64, 65], Minecraft learning algorithms can be divided into two categories: 1) Low-level controller: Many prior efforts leverage hierarchical reinforcement learning to learn from human demonstrations [66, 67, 68]. Kanitscheider et al. [14] design a curriculum based on success rates, but its objectives are limited to curated items. MineDojo [23] and VPT [8] utilize YouTube videos for large-scale pre-training. DreamerV3 [69], on the other hand, learns a world model to explore the environment and collect diamonds. 2) High-level planner: Volum et al. [70] leverage few-shot prompting with Codex [41] to generate executable policies, but they require additional human interaction. Recent works leverage LLMs as a high-level planner in Minecraft by decomposing a high-level task into several subgoals following Minecraft recipes [55, 53, 71], thus lacking full exploration flexibility. Like these latter works, Voyager also uses LLMs as a high-level planner by prompting GPT-4 and utilizes Mineflayer [52] as a low-level controller following Volum et al. [70]. Unlike prior works, Voyager employs an automatic curriculum that unfolds in a bottom-up manner, driven by curiosity, and therefore enables open-ended exploration.
paras.zh: Minecraft 是一个开放式的 3D 世界，具有极其灵活的游戏机制，支持广泛的活动。基于著名的 Minecraft 基准测试 [23, 61, 62, 63, 64, 65]，Minecraft 学习算法可分为两类：1) 低级控制器：许多先前的工作利用分层强化学习从人类演示中学习 [66, 67, 68]。Kanitscheider 等人 [14] 设计了一个基于成功率的课程，但其目标仅限于精选物品。MineDojo [23] 和 VPT [8] 利用 YouTube 视频进行大规模预训练。另一方面，DreamerV3 [69] 学习一个世界模型来探索环境并收集钻石。2) 高级规划器：Volum 等人 [70] 利用 Codex [41] 的少样本提示生成可执行策略，但需要额外的人类交互。最近的工作利用 LLM 作为 Minecraft 中的高级规划器，通过将高级任务分解为遵循 Minecraft 配方的多个子目标 [55, 53, 71]，因此缺乏完全的探索灵活性。与这些后期工作类似，Voyager 也使用 LLM 作为高级规划器，通过提示 GPT-4，并利用 Mineflayer [52] 作为低级控制器，遵循 Volum 等人 [70] 的方法。与先前工作不同，Voyager 采用了一种自动课程，该课程以自下而上的方式展开，由好奇心驱动，因此实现了开放式探索。

paras.en: Inspired by the strong emergent capabilities of LLMs, such as zero-shot prompting and complex reasoning [72, 37, 38, 36, 73, 74], embodied agent research [75, 76, 77, 78] has witnessed a significant increase in the utilization of LLMs for planning purposes. Recent efforts can be roughly classified into two groups. 1) Large language models for robot learning: Many prior works apply LLMs to generate subgoals for robot planning [27, 27, 25, 79, 80]. Inner Monologue [26] incorporates environment feedback for robot planning with LLMs. Code as Policies [16] and ProgPrompt [22] directly leverage LLMs to generate executable robot policies. VIMA [19] and PaLM-E [59] fine-tune pre-trained LLMs to support multimodal prompts. 2) Large language models for text agents: ReAct [29] leverages chain-of-thought prompting [46] and generates both reasoning traces and task-specific actions with LLMs. Reflexion [30] is built upon ReAct [29] with self-reflection to enhance reasoning. AutoGPT [28] is a popular tool that automates NLP tasks by crafting a curriculum of multiple subgoals for completing a high-level goal while incorporating ReAct [29]’s reasoning and acting loops. DERA [81] frames a task as a dialogue between two GPT-4 [35] agents. Generative Agents [82] leverages ChatGPT [50] to simulate human behaviors by storing agents’ experiences as memories and retrieving those for planning, but its agent actions are not executable. SPRING [83] is a concurrent work that uses GPT-4 to extract game mechanics from game manuals, based on which it answers questions arranged in a directed acyclic graph and predicts the next action. All these works lack a skill library for developing more complex behaviors, which are crucial components for the success of Voyager in lifelong learning.
paras.zh: 受 LLM 强大的涌现能力（如零样本提示和复杂推理 [72, 37, 38, 36, 73, 74]）的启发，具身智能体研究 [75, 76, 77, 78] 中利用 LLM 进行规划的情况显著增加。最近的工作大致可分为两类。1) 用于机器人学习的大语言模型：许多先前的工作应用 LLM 为机器人规划生成子目标 [27, 27, 25, 79, 80]。Inner Monologue [26] 结合环境反馈用于 LLM 的机器人规划。Code as Policies [16] 和 ProgPrompt [22] 直接利用 LLM 生成可执行的机器人策略。VIMA [19] 和 PaLM-E [59] 微调预训练的 LLM 以支持多模态提示。2) 用于文本智能体的大语言模型：ReAct [29] 利用思维链提示 [46]，并使用 LLM 生成推理轨迹和特定任务的动作。Reflexion [30] 基于 ReAct [29] 构建，通过自我反思增强推理。AutoGPT [28] 是一种流行工具，通过制定多个子目标的课程来完成高级目标，同时结合 ReAct [29] 的推理和行动循环，自动化 NLP 任务。DERA [81] 将任务框架化为两个 GPT-4 [35] 智能体之间的对话。Generative Agents [82] 利用 ChatGPT [50] 模拟人类行为，通过存储智能体的经验作为记忆并检索这些记忆进行规划，但其智能体动作不可执行。SPRING [83] 是一项并行工作，使用 GPT-4 从游戏手册中提取游戏机制，基于此回答按有向无环图排列的问题，并预测下一个动作。所有这些工作都缺乏用于开发更复杂行为的技能库，而技能库是 Voyager 在终身学习中成功的关键组成部分。

paras.en: Code generation has been a longstanding challenge in NLP [41, 84, 85, 73, 37], with various works leveraging execution results to improve program synthesis. Execution-guided approaches leverage intermediate execution outcomes to guide program search [86, 87, 88]. Another line of research utilizes majority voting to choose candidates based on their execution performance [89, 90]. Additionally, LEVER [91] trains a verifier to distinguish and reject incorrect programs based on execution results. CLAIRIFY [92], on the other hand, generates code for planning chemistry experiments and makes use of a rule-based verifier to iteratively provide error feedback to LLMs. Voyager distinguishes itself from these works by integrating environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification (to assess task success) into an iterative prompting mechanism for embodied control.
paras.zh: 代码生成一直是 NLP 中的一个长期挑战 [41, 84, 85, 73, 37]，各种工作利用执行结果来改进程序合成。执行引导的方法利用中间执行结果来指导程序搜索 [86, 87, 88]。另一条研究线利用多数投票根据执行性能选择候选 [89, 90]。此外，LEVER [91] 训练一个验证器，根据执行结果区分和拒绝不正确的程序。另一方面，CLAIRIFY [92] 为规划化学实验生成代码，并利用基于规则的验证器迭代地向 LLM 提供错误反馈。Voyager 与这些工作的不同之处在于，它将环境反馈、执行错误和自我验证（用于评估任务成功）整合到一个用于具身控制的迭代提示机制中。

paras.en: In this work, we introduce Voyager, the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent, which leverages GPT-4 to explore the world continuously, develop increasingly sophisticated skills, and make new discoveries consistently without human intervention. Voyager exhibits superior performance in discovering novel items, unlocking the Minecraft tech tree, traversing diverse terrains, and applying its learned skill library to unseen tasks in a newly instantiated world. Voyager serves as a starting point to develop powerful generalist agents without tuning the model parameters.
paras.zh: 在这项工作中，我们介绍了Voyager，这是首个由LLM驱动的具身终身学习智能体，它利用GPT-4持续探索世界、发展日益复杂的技能，并在无需人工干预的情况下不断做出新发现。Voyager在发现新物品、解锁Minecraft科技树、穿越多样地形以及将其学到的技能库应用于新实例化世界中的未见任务方面表现出卓越性能。Voyager为开发无需调整模型参数的强大通用智能体提供了一个起点。

paras.en: Our research is conducted within Minecraft, a safe and harmless 3D video game environment. While Voyager is designed to be generally applicable to other domains, such as robotics, its application to physical robots would require additional attention and the implementation of safety constraints by humans to ensure responsible and secure deployment.
paras.zh: 我们的研究在Minecraft中进行，这是一个安全无害的3D视频游戏环境。虽然Voyager被设计为可普遍适用于其他领域，例如机器人技术，但其在物理机器人上的应用需要额外关注，并由人类实施安全约束，以确保负责任和安全的部署。

paras.en: We are extremely grateful to Ziming Zhu, Kaiyu Yang, Rafał Kocielnik, Colin White, Or Sharir, Sahin Lale, De-An Huang, Jean Kossaifi, Yuncong Yang, Charles Zhang, Minchao Huang, and many other colleagues and friends for their helpful feedback and insightful discussions. This work is done during Guanzhi Wang’s internship at NVIDIA. Guanzhi Wang is supported by the Kortschak fellowship in Computing and Mathematical Sciences at Caltech.
paras.zh: 我们非常感谢朱子明、杨凯宇、Rafał Kocielnik、Colin White、Or Sharir、Sahin Lale、黄德安、Jean Kossaifi、杨云聪、Charles Zhang、黄敏超以及许多其他同事和朋友提供的宝贵反馈和富有洞见的讨论。本工作是在Guanzhi Wang于NVIDIA实习期间完成的。Guanzhi Wang得到了加州理工学院计算与数学科学系的Kortschak奖学金支持。

paras.en: GPT-4 and GPT-3.5 offer users the ability to designate the role of each prompt message among three options:
paras.zh: GPT-4 和 GPT-3.5 允许用户从三个选项中指定每条提示消息的角色：

paras.en: System: A high-level instruction that guides the model behavior throughout the conversation. It sets the overall tone and objective for the interaction.
paras.zh: 系统：一个高级指令，用于在整个对话中指导模型行为。它设定了交互的整体基调和目标。

paras.en: User: A detailed instruction that guides the assistant for the next immediate response.
paras.zh: 用户：一个详细指令，用于指导助手立即生成下一个响应。

paras.en: Assistant: A response message generated the model.
paras.zh: 助手：模型生成的响应消息。

paras.en: See https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/chat/introduction for more details.
paras.zh: 更多详情请参见 https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/chat/introduction。

paras.en: To save token usage, instead of engaging in multi-round conversations, we concatenate a system prompt and a user prompt to obtain each assistant’s response.
paras.zh: 为节省 token 使用量，我们不进行多轮对话，而是将系统提示和用户提示拼接起来，以获取每个助手的响应。

paras.en: The input prompt to GPT-4 consists of several components:
paras.zh: GPT-4 的输入提示由几个部分组成：

paras.en: Directives encouraging diverse behaviors and imposing constraints (so that the proposed task is achievable and verifiable): See Sec. A.3.4 for the full prompt;
paras.zh: 鼓励多样化行为并施加约束（以便提出的任务可实现且可验证）的指令：完整提示见第 A.3.4 节；

paras.en: Inventory: A dictionary of items with counts, for example, {‘cobblestone’: 4, ‘furnace’: 1, ‘stone_pickaxe’: 1, ‘oak_planks’: 7, ‘dirt’: 6, ‘wooden_pickaxe’: 1, ‘crafting_table’: 1, ‘raw_iron’: 4, ‘coal’: 1};
paras.zh: 库存：一个包含物品及其数量的字典，例如，{‘cobblestone’: 4, ‘furnace’: 1, ‘stone_pickaxe’: 1, ‘oak_planks’: 7, ‘dirt’: 6, ‘wooden_pickaxe’: 1, ‘crafting_table’: 1, ‘raw_iron’: 4, ‘coal’: 1}；

paras.en: Equipment: Armors or weapons equipped by the agents;
paras.zh: 装备：智能体装备的盔甲或武器；

paras.en: Nearby blocks: A set of block names within a 32-block distance to the agent, for example, ‘dirt’, ‘water’, ‘spruce_planks’, ‘grass_block’, ‘dirt_path’, ‘sugar_cane’, ‘fern’;
paras.zh: 附近方块：智能体 32 格距离内的一组方块名称，例如，‘dirt’, ‘water’, ‘spruce_planks’, ‘grass_block’, ‘dirt_path’, ‘sugar_cane’, ‘fern’；

paras.en: Other blocks that are recently seen: Blocks that are not nearby or in the inventory;
paras.zh: 最近看到的其他方块：不在附近或库存中的方块；

paras.en: Nearby entities: A set of entity names within a 32-block distance to the agent, for example, ‘pig’, ‘cat’, ‘villager’, ‘zombie’;
paras.zh: 附近实体：智能体 32 格距离内的一组实体名称，例如，‘pig’, ‘cat’, ‘villager’, ‘zombie’；

paras.en: A list of chests that are seen by the agent: Chests are external containers where the agent can deposit items. If a chest is not opened before, its content is “Unknown”. Otherwise, the items inside each chest are shown to the agent.
paras.zh: 智能体看到的箱子列表：箱子是外部容器，智能体可以存放物品。如果箱子之前未被打开，其内容为“未知”。否则，每个箱子内的物品会展示给智能体。

paras.en: Biome: For example, ‘plains’, ‘flower_forest’, ‘meadow’, ‘river’, ‘beach’, ‘forest’, ‘snowy_slopes’, ‘frozen_peaks’, ‘old_growth_birch_forest’, ‘ocean’, ‘sunflower_plains’, ‘stony_shore’;
paras.zh: 生物群系：例如，‘plains’, ‘flower_forest’, ‘meadow’, ‘river’, ‘beach’, ‘forest’, ‘snowy_slopes’, ‘frozen_peaks’, ‘old_growth_birch_forest’, ‘ocean’, ‘sunflower_plains’, ‘stony_shore’；

paras.en: Time: One of ‘sunrise’, ‘day’, ‘noon’, ‘sunset’, ‘night’, ‘midnight’;
paras.zh: 时间：取值为‘sunrise’, ‘day’, ‘noon’, ‘sunset’, ‘night’, ‘midnight’之一；

paras.en: Health and hunger bars: The max value is 20;
paras.zh: 生命值和饥饿值条：最大值为 20；

paras.en: Position: 3D coordinate (x,y,z)𝑥𝑦𝑧(x,y,z) of the agent’s position in the Minecraft world;
paras.zh: 位置：智能体在 Minecraft 世界中的三维坐标 (x,y,z)；

paras.en: Chain-of-thought prompting [46] in response: We request GPT-4 to first reason about the current progress and then suggest the next task.
paras.zh: 响应中的思维链提示 [46]：我们要求 GPT-4 首先推理当前进度，然后提出下一个任务。

paras.en: We leverage GPT-3.5 to self-ask questions to provide additional context. Each question is paired with a concept that is used for retrieving the most relevant document from the wiki knowledge base [23]. We feed the document content to GPT-3.5 for self-answering questions. In practice, using a wiki knowledge base is optional since GPT-3.5 already possesses a good understanding of Minecraft game mechanics. However, the external knowledge base becomes advantageous if GPT-3.5 is not pre-trained in that specific domain. See Sec. A.3.4 for the full prompt.
paras.zh: 我们利用 GPT-3.5 自我提问以提供额外上下文。每个问题都配有一个概念，用于从 wiki 知识库 [23] 中检索最相关的文档。我们将文档内容输入 GPT-3.5 以自我回答问题。实践中，使用 wiki 知识库是可选的，因为 GPT-3.5 已经对 Minecraft 游戏机制有很好的理解。然而，如果 GPT-3.5 在特定领域未经过预训练，外部知识库就会变得有利。完整提示见第 A.3.4 节。

paras.en: In practice, we adopt a warm-up schedule to gradually incorporate the agent’s state and the additional context into the prompt based on how many tasks the agent has completed. This ensures that the prompt is exposed to increasing amounts of information over the exploration progress and therefore begins with basic skills and progressively advances towards more intricate and diverse ones. The warm-up setting that we use across all the experiments is shown in Table. A.1.
paras.zh: 在实践中，我们采用热身计划，根据智能体已完成的任务数量，逐步将智能体状态和额外上下文纳入提示中。这确保了提示在探索过程中逐渐暴露于越来越多的信息，从而从基本技能开始，逐步推进到更复杂和多样化的技能。我们在所有实验中使用的热身设置如表 A.1 所示。

paras.en: The input prompt to GPT-4 consists of the following components:
paras.zh: 输入给 GPT-4 的提示词包含以下组成部分：

paras.en: Guidelines for code generation: See Sec A.4.2 for the full prompt;
paras.zh: 代码生成指南：完整提示词见第 A.4.2 节；

paras.en: Control primitive APIs implemented by us: These APIs serve a dual purpose: they demonstrate the usage of Mineflayer APIs, and they can be directly called by GPT-4.
paras.zh: 我们实现的控制原语 API：这些 API 有双重用途：它们展示了 Mineflayer API 的用法，并且可以直接被 GPT-4 调用。

paras.en: exploreUntil(bot, direction, maxTime = 60, callback): Allow the agent to explore in a fixed direction for maxTime. The callback is the stopping condition implemented by the agent to determine when to stop exploring;
paras.zh: exploreUntil(bot, direction, maxTime = 60, callback)：让智能体在固定方向上探索 maxTime 时间。callback 是由智能体实现的停止条件，用于决定何时停止探索；

paras.en: mineBlock(bot, name, count = 1): Mine and collect the specified number of blocks within a 32-block distance;
paras.zh: mineBlock(bot, name, count = 1)：在 32 格距离内挖掘并收集指定数量的方块；

paras.en: craftItem(bot, name, count = 1): Craft the item with a crafting table nearby;
paras.zh: craftItem(bot, name, count = 1)：在附近有工作台的情况下合成物品；

paras.en: placeItem(bot, name, position): Place the block at the specified position;
paras.zh: placeItem(bot, name, position)：在指定位置放置方块；

paras.en: smeltItem(bot, itemName, fuelName, count = 1): Smelt the item with the specified fuel. There must be a furnace nearby;
paras.zh: smeltItem(bot, itemName, fuelName, count = 1)：使用指定燃料冶炼物品。附近必须有熔炉；

paras.en: killMob(bot, mobName, timeout = 300): Attack the mob and collect its dropped item;
paras.zh: killMob(bot, mobName, timeout = 300)：攻击生物并收集其掉落物；

paras.en: getItemFromChest(bot, chestPosition, itemsToGet): Move to the chest at the specified position and get items from the chest;
paras.zh: getItemFromChest(bot, chestPosition, itemsToGet)：移动到指定位置的箱子并从箱子中取出物品；

paras.en: depositItemIntoChest(bot, chestPosition, itemsToDeposit): Move to the chest at the specified position and deposit items into the chest;
paras.zh: depositItemIntoChest(bot, chestPosition, itemsToDeposit)：移动到指定位置的箱子并将物品存入箱子；

paras.en: Control primitive APIs provided by Mineflayer:
paras.zh: Mineflayer 提供的控制原语 API：

paras.en: await bot.pathfinder.goto(goal): Go to a specific position. See below for how to set the goal;
paras.zh: await bot.pathfinder.goto(goal)：前往指定位置。关于如何设置目标，见下文；

paras.en: new GoalNear(x, y, z, range): Move the bot to a block within the specified range of the specified block;
paras.zh: new GoalNear(x, y, z, range)：将机器人移动到指定方块指定范围内的某个方块；

paras.en: new GoalXZ(x, z): For long-range goals that don’t have a specific Y level;
paras.zh: new GoalXZ(x, z)：用于没有特定 Y 层高度的远距离目标；

paras.en: new GoalGetToBlock(x, y, z): Not get into the block, but get directly adjacent to it. Useful for fishing, farming, filling a bucket, and using a bed.;
paras.zh: new GoalGetToBlock(x, y, z)：不进入方块，而是直接相邻。适用于钓鱼、耕种、填充水桶和使用床；

paras.en: new GoalFollow(entity, range): Follow the specified entity within the specified range;
paras.zh: new GoalFollow(entity, range)：在指定范围内跟随指定实体；

paras.en: new GoalPlaceBlock(position, bot.world, {}): Position the bot in order to place a block;
paras.zh: new GoalPlaceBlock(position, bot.world, {})：定位机器人以便放置方块；

paras.en: new GoalLookAtBlock(position, bot.world, {}): Path towards a position where a face of the block at position is visible;
paras.zh: new GoalLookAtBlock(position, bot.world, {})：路径规划到能看到指定方块某个面的位置；

paras.en: bot.isABed(bedBlock): Return true if bedBlock is a bed;
paras.zh: bot.isABed(bedBlock)：如果 bedBlock 是床则返回 true；

paras.en: bot.blockAt(position): Return the block at position;
paras.zh: bot.blockAt(position)：返回指定位置的方块；

paras.en: await bot.equip(item, destination): Equip the item in the specified destination. destination must be one of “hand”, “head”, “torso”, “legs”, “feet”, “off-hand”;
paras.zh: await bot.equip(item, destination)：将物品装备到指定位置。destination 必须是“hand”、“head”、“torso”、“legs”、“feet”、“off-hand”之一；

paras.en: await bot.consume(): Consume the item in the bot’s hand. You must equip the item to consume first. Useful for eating food, drinking potions, etc.;
paras.zh: await bot.consume()：消耗机器人手中的物品。必须先装备要消耗的物品。适用于吃食物、喝药水等；

paras.en: await bot.fish(): Let bot fish. Before calling this function, you must first get to a water block and then equip a fishing rod. The bot will automatically stop fishing when it catches a fish;
paras.zh: await bot.fish()：让机器人钓鱼。调用此函数前，必须先到达水源方块并装备钓鱼竿。机器人钓到鱼时会自动停止；

paras.en: await bot.sleep(bedBlock): Sleep until sunrise. You must get to a bed block first;
paras.zh: await bot.sleep(bedBlock)：睡觉直到日出。必须先到达床方块；

paras.en: await bot.activateBlock(block): This is the same as right-clicking a block in the game. Useful for buttons, doors, etc. You must get to the block first;
paras.zh: await bot.activateBlock(block)：相当于游戏中右键点击方块。适用于按钮、门等。必须先到达方块；

paras.en: await bot.lookAt(position): Look at the specified position. You must go near the position before you look at it. To fill a bucket with water, you must look at it first;
paras.zh: await bot.lookAt(position)：看向指定位置。必须先靠近该位置。要用水桶装水，必须先看向水；

paras.en: await bot.activateItem(): This is the same as right-clicking to use the item in the bot’s hand. Useful for using a bucket, etc. You must equip the item to activate first;
paras.zh: await bot.activateItem()：相当于右键使用机器人手中的物品。适用于使用水桶等。必须先装备要激活的物品；

paras.en: await bot.useOn(entity): This is the same as right-clicking an entity in the game. Useful for shearing a sheep. You must get to the entity first;
paras.zh: await bot.useOn(entity)：相当于游戏中右键点击实体。适用于剪羊毛。必须先到达实体；

paras.en: Environment feedback: The chat log in the prompt;
paras.zh: 环境反馈：提示词中的聊天日志；

paras.en: Critique from the self-verification module;
paras.zh: 来自自我验证模块的批评；

paras.en: The agent’s current state: See Sec. A.3.1 for each element of the agent’s state;
paras.zh: 智能体的当前状态：智能体状态的每个元素见第 A.3.1 节；

paras.en: Task proposed by the automatic curriculum;
paras.zh: 自动课程提出的任务；

paras.en: Task context: We prompt GPT-3.5 to ask for general suggestions about how to solve the task. In practice, this part is handled by the automatic curriculum since it has a systematic mechanism for question-answering (Sec. A.3.2);
paras.zh: 任务上下文：我们提示 GPT-3.5 询问关于如何解决任务的一般性建议。实际上，这部分由自动课程处理，因为它有系统的问答机制（第 A.3.2 节）；

paras.en: Chain-of-thought prompting [46] in response: We ask GPT-4 to first explain the reason why the code from the last round fails, then give step-by-step plans to finish the task, and finally generate code. See Sec. A.4.2 for the full prompt.
paras.zh: 响应中的思维链提示 [46]：我们要求 GPT-4 首先解释上一轮代码失败的原因，然后给出完成任务的逐步计划，最后生成代码。完整提示词见第 A.4.2 节。

paras.en: The input prompt to GPT-4 consists of the following components:
paras.zh: GPT-4 的输入提示由以下部分组成：

paras.en: The agent’s state: We exclude other blocks that are recently seen and nearby entities from the agent’s state since they are not useful for assessing the task’s completeness. See Sec. A.3.1 for each element of the agent’s state;
paras.zh: 智能体的状态：我们从智能体状态中排除了最近看到和附近的其他方块，因为它们对评估任务完成情况没有帮助。智能体状态的每个元素见第 A.3.1 节；

paras.en: Task proposed by the automatic curriculum;
paras.zh: 自动课程提出的任务；

paras.en: Task context: We prompt GPT-3.5 to ask for general suggestions about how to solve the task. In practice, this part is handled by the automatic curriculum since it has a systematic mechanism for question-answering (Sec. A.3.2);
paras.zh: 任务上下文：我们提示 GPT-3.5 询问关于如何解决任务的一般建议。实际上，这部分由自动课程处理，因为它有一个系统的问答机制（第 A.3.2 节）；

paras.en: Chain-of-thought prompting [46] in response: We request GPT-4 to initially reason about the task’s success or failure, then output a boolean variable indicating the task’s outcome, and finally provide a critique to the agent if the task fails.
paras.zh: 响应中的思维链提示 [46]：我们要求 GPT-4 首先推理任务的成功或失败，然后输出一个布尔变量指示任务结果，最后在任务失败时向智能体提供批评。

paras.en: Few-shot examples for in-context learning [36, 37, 38].
paras.zh: 用于上下文学习的少样本示例 [36, 37, 38]。

full.sec: A.6 System-level Comparison between Voyager and Prior Works
full.secZh: A.6 Voyager 与先前工作的系统级比较

paras.en: We make a system-level comparison in Table. A.2. Voyager stands out as the only method featuring a combination of automatic curriculum, iterative planning, and a skill library. Moreover, it learns to play Minecraft without the need for any gradient update.
paras.zh: 我们在表 A.2 中进行了系统级比较。Voyager 是唯一一种结合了自动课程、迭代规划和技能库的方法。此外，它无需任何梯度更新即可学习玩 Minecraft。

paras.en: Our simulation environment is built upon MineDojo [23] and utilizes Mineflayer [52] JavaScript APIs for motor controls (Sec. A.4.2). Additionally, we incorporate many bot.chat() into Mineflayer functions to provide abundant environment feedback and implement various condition checks along with try-catch exceptions for continuous execution. If the bot dies, it is resurrected near the closest ground, and its inventory is preserved for uninterrupted exploration. The bot recycles its crafting table and furnace after program execution. For detailed implementations, please refer to our codebase.
paras.zh: 我们的仿真环境基于MineDojo [23]构建，并利用Mineflayer [52] JavaScript API进行运动控制（详见第A.4.2节）。此外，我们在Mineflayer函数中嵌入了大量bot.chat()调用，以提供丰富的环境反馈，并通过各种条件检查以及try-catch异常处理实现持续执行。若机器人死亡，它将在最近的地面附近复活，其库存得以保留以支持不间断探索。机器人在程序执行后会回收其工作台和熔炉。具体实现细节请参考我们的代码库。

paras.en: ReAct [29] uses chain-of-thought prompting [46] by generating both reasoning traces and action plans with LLMs. We provide it with our environment feedback and the agent states as observations. ReAct undergoes one round of code generation from scratch, followed by three rounds of code refinement. This process is then repeated until the maximum prompting iteration is reached.
paras.zh: ReAct [29] 使用思维链提示 [46]，通过大语言模型同时生成推理轨迹和行动计划。我们为其提供环境反馈和智能体状态作为观察。ReAct 经历一轮从头开始的代码生成，随后进行三轮代码优化。此过程重复进行，直到达到最大提示迭代次数。

paras.en: Reflexion [30] is built on top of ReAct [29] with self-reflection to infer more intuitive future actions. We provide it with environment feedback, the agent states, execution errors, and our self-verification module. Similar to ReAct, Reflexion undergoes one round of code generation from scratch, followed by three rounds of code refinement. This process is then repeated until the maximum prompting iteration is reached.
paras.zh: Reflexion [30] 建立在 ReAct [29] 之上，通过自我反思推断更直观的未来行动。我们为其提供环境反馈、智能体状态、执行错误以及我们的自我验证模块。与 ReAct 类似，Reflexion 经历一轮从头开始的代码生成，随后进行三轮代码优化。此过程重复进行，直到达到最大提示迭代次数。

paras.en: AutoGPT [28] is a popular software tool that automates NLP tasks by decomposing a high-level goal into multiple subgoals and executing them in a ReAct-style loop. We re-implement AutoGPT by using GPT-4 to do task decomposition and provide it with the agent states, environment feedback, and execution errors as observations for subgoal execution. Compared with Voyager, AutoGPT lacks the skill library for accumulating knowledge, self-verification for assessing task success, and automatic curriculum for open-ended exploration. During each subgoal execution, if no execution error occurs, we consider the subgoal completed and proceed to the next one. Otherwise, we refine the program until three rounds of code refinement (equivalent to four rounds of code generation) are completed, and then move on to the next subgoal. If three consecutive subgoals do not result in acquiring a new item, we replan by rerunning the task decomposition.
paras.zh: AutoGPT [28] 是一种流行的软件工具，通过将高层次目标分解为多个子目标并以 ReAct 风格的循环执行它们来自动化自然语言处理任务。我们重新实现了 AutoGPT，使用 GPT-4 进行任务分解，并为其提供智能体状态、环境反馈和执行错误作为子目标执行的观察。与 Voyager 相比，AutoGPT 缺乏用于积累知识的技能库、用于评估任务成功的自我验证以及用于开放式探索的自动课程。在每个子目标执行期间，如果没有发生执行错误，我们认为子目标完成并继续下一个。否则，我们优化程序直到完成三轮代码优化（相当于四轮代码生成），然后继续下一个子目标。如果连续三个子目标没有获得新物品，我们通过重新运行任务分解来重新规划。

paras.en: The task is “explore the world and get as many items as possible” for all baselines.
paras.zh: 所有基线的任务是“探索世界并尽可能多地获取物品”。

paras.en: We ablate 6 design choices (automatic curriculum, skill library, environment feedback, execution errors, self-verification, and GPT-4 for code generation) in Voyager and study their impact on exploration performance.
paras.zh: 我们对 Voyager 中的 6 个设计选择（自动课程、技能库、环境反馈、执行错误、自我验证和用于代码生成的 GPT-4）进行了消融实验，并研究了它们对探索性能的影响。

paras.en: Manual Curriculum: We substitute the automatic curriculum with a manually designed curriculum for mining a diamond: “Mine 3 wood log”, “Craft 1 crafting table”, “Craft 1 wooden pickaxe”, “Mine 11 cobblestone”, “Craft 1 stone pickaxe”, “Craft 1 furnace”, “Mine 3 iron ore”, “Smelt 3 iron ore”, “Craft 1 iron pickaxe”, “Mine 1 diamond”. A manual curriculum requires human effort to design and is not scalable for open-ended exploration.
paras.zh: 手动课程：我们将自动课程替换为用于挖掘钻石的手动设计课程：“砍 3 根原木”、“制作 1 个工作台”、“制作 1 把木镐”、“挖 11 个圆石”、“制作 1 把石镐”、“制作 1 个熔炉”、“挖 3 个铁矿石”、“熔炼 3 个铁矿石”、“制作 1 把铁镐”、“挖 1 个钻石”。手动课程需要人工设计，且对于开放式探索不可扩展。

paras.en: Random Curriculum: We curate 101 items obtained by Voyager and create a random curriculum by randomly selecting one item as the next task.
paras.zh: 随机课程：我们整理了 Voyager 获得的 101 个物品，并通过随机选择一个物品作为下一个任务来创建随机课程。

paras.en: w/o Skill Library: We remove the skill library, eliminating skill retrieval for code generation.
paras.zh: 无技能库：我们移除了技能库，消除了代码生成中的技能检索。

paras.en: w/o Environment Feedback: We exclude environment feedback (chat log) from the prompt for code generation.
paras.zh: 无环境反馈：我们从代码生成的提示中排除了环境反馈（聊天日志）。

paras.en: w/o Execution Errors: We exclude execution errors from the prompt for code generation.
paras.zh: 无执行错误：我们从代码生成的提示中排除了执行错误。

paras.en: w/o Self-Verification: For each task, we generate code without self-verification and iteratively refine the program for 3 rounds (equivalent to 4 rounds of code generation in total).
paras.zh: 无自我验证：对于每个任务，我们在没有自我验证的情况下生成代码，并迭代优化程序 3 轮（总共相当于 4 轮代码生成）。

paras.en: GPT-3.5: We replace GPT-4 with GPT-3.5 for code generation. We retain GPT-4 for the automatic curriculum and the self-verification module.
paras.zh: GPT-3.5：我们将代码生成的 GPT-4 替换为 GPT-3.5。我们保留 GPT-4 用于自动课程和自我验证模块。

paras.en: The results of zero-shot generalization to unseen tasks for the other two tasks are presented in Fig. A.3. Similar to Fig. 8, Voyager consistently solves all tasks, while the baselines are unable to solve any task within 50 prompting iterations. Our skill library, constructed from lifelong learning, not only enhances Voyager’s performance but also provides a boost to AutoGPT [28].
paras.zh: 另外两个任务的零样本泛化到未见任务的结果如图A.3所示。与图8类似，Voyager始终能解决所有任务，而基线方法在50次提示迭代内无法解决任何任务。我们从终身学习中构建的技能库不仅提升了Voyager的性能，也为AutoGPT [28]带来了提升。

paras.en: We conduct an evaluation of our skill retrieval (309 samples in total) and the results are in Table. A.4. The top-5 accuracy standing at 96.5% suggests our retrieval process is reliable (note that we include the top-5 relevant skills in the prompt for synthesizing a new skill).
paras.zh: 我们对技能检索（共309个样本）进行了评估，结果见表A.4。前5准确率达到96.5%，表明我们的检索过程是可靠的（注意，我们在合成新技能的提示中包含了前5个相关技能）。

paras.en: In the main paper, all of Voyager’s experiments are conducted with gpt-4-0314. We additionally run new experiments with gpt-4-0613 and find that the performance is roughly the same (Fig. A.4). It demonstrates that Voyager is robust to model variations.
paras.zh: 在正文中，Voyager的所有实验均使用gpt-4-0314进行。我们额外使用gpt-4-0613进行了新实验，发现性能大致相同（图A.4）。这表明Voyager对模型变化具有鲁棒性。
